preview_text:
The model rates Norway as a clear favorite with a 65% win probability, while Iraq has an 11% chance of victory. Norway's advantage over the underdog spans more than half a century of forecast probability, reflecting a substantial expected gap in match outcome.

The draw probability of 24% indicates a moderately competitive fixture despite the favorite's dominance. That draw likelihood suggests the margin between the teams is not so wide as to make a single-goal result implausible, though Norway is expected to control the contest more often than not. A stalemate remains the second-most-probable outcome in the model's distribution.

The market consensus diverges meaningfully from the model. Sportsbooks push Norway's win probability to 79%, a gap of roughly 14 percentage points above the model's estimate, while depressing both the draw (14%) and Iraq's chances (7%) below model levels. Polymarket prices align even more sharply in Norway's favor at 81%. The dashboard does not adjudicate between these sources; the gap suggests market and model weight the teams' relative strength differently.
